title: Ghanteshwar Temple
tagline: Ghanteshwar Temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Ghanteshwar temple is situated at Ubidpur Khanakul (near Khanakul I block office) Hooghly, West Bengal India on the bank of river Ratnakar, which is now virtually dead.
overview.description: Ghanteshwar temple is situated at Ubidpur Khanakul (near Khanakul I block office) Hooghly, West Bengal India on the bank of river Ratnakar, which is now virtually dead. Lord Shiva is the mainly worshipped deity here. The temple complex also hosts many other deities like Durga, Kaal Bhairav, Surya, Kaali, Shashti, Sitala, Dharmaraj, Radha Krishna, Annapurna, Shani etc. It also hosts a shrine for Sufi Pir (Peer) Baba.

topPlaces.name: Khanakul I
topPlaces.description: Khanakul I is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Arambag subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Radhanagore
topPlaces.description: Radhanagore (also spelled Radhanagar) is a village in Khanakul I CD block of Arambagh subdivision in the Hooghly District of West Bengal, India. It is the birthplace of Ram Mohan Roy.

topPlaces.name: Khanakul
topPlaces.description: Khanakul is a village in Khanakul I CD block of Hooghly district in the state of West Bengal, India. Khanakul is the birthplace of Raja Rammohan Roy.

topPlaces.name: Natibpur, Hooghly
topPlaces.description: Marokhana is a village in the Khanakul II CD block in the Arambagh subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Khanakul II
topPlaces.description: Khanakul II is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Arambag subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Gourhati
topPlaces.description: Gourhati is a village in the Arambagh CD block in the Arambagh subdivision of the Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Shilabati River
topPlaces.description: Shilabati River (also known as Shilai) originates near Chak Gopalpur village of Hura block in the Purulia district of the Indian state of West Bengal. It flows in an almost southeasterly direction through the districts of Bankura and Paschim Medinipur.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Hooghly
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Hooghly or locally known as JNV Dihibagnan is a boarding, co-educational school in Hooghly district of West Bengal in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the ministry.

items.note: Khanakul I is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Arambag subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.
Radhanagore (also spelled Radhanagar) is a village in Khanakul I CD block of Arambagh subdivision in the Hooghly District of West Bengal, India. It is the birthplace of Ram Mohan Roy.
Khanakul is a village in Khanakul I CD block of Hooghly district in the state of West Bengal, India. Khanakul is the birthplace of Raja Rammohan Roy.
Marokhana is a village in the Khanakul II CD block in the Arambagh subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

Gourhati is a village in the Arambagh CD block in the Arambagh subdivision of the Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

Shilabati River (also known as Shilai) originates near Chak Gopalpur village of Hura block in the Purulia district of the Indian state of West Bengal. It flows in an almost southeasterly direction through the districts of Bankura and Paschim Medinipur.

Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Hooghly or locally known as JNV Dihibagnan is a boarding, co-educational school in Hooghly district of West Bengal in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the ministry.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ghanteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ghanteshwar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ghanteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (61 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (52 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ghanteshwar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ghanteshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ghanteshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Ghanteshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ghanteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Khanakul I, Radhanagore, Khanakul, Natibpur, Hooghly, Khanakul II.
